content.text: I can see that on line 126-128, there's a check for `flushSync()`. The issue says that in version 5.36.0-5.36.13, styles are not synchronously available but in 5.35.7 they were. Let me check the git history for this file:

content.text: That's a huge commit. Let me check if the async_mode_flag is related to the issue. Let me look at the specific change to the legacy-client.js:

input.description: Check flushSync logic in that commit

content.text: Now let me check what it was before:

input.description: Check flushSync logic before that commit
Look for custom element related commits

content.content:
9d1aa699b Docs: Updated the custom elements documentation to include $host (#16686)
6534aa08e fix: Add check for builtin custom elements in `set_custom_element_data` (#16592)
37488fa65 fix: warn when using rest or identifier in custom elements without props option (#16003)
8e21c41c2 Link from $host directive docs to custom elements docs (#15686)
4285bf9c7 docs: move $host docs to custom elements page
3b7066f5c fix: ensure custom element updates don't run in hydration mode (#15217)
6f392d679 fix: properly set `value` property of custom elements (#15206)
34628b9c9 docs: note custom elements on caveat (#14934)
2e2b44095 fix: apply `clsx` logic to custom element `class` attributes (#14907)
c05abb5e5 fix: don't delegate events on custom elements, solve edge case stopPropagation issue
a2539cfe1 fix: correctly set custom element props (#14508)
7d11fa8da fix: recognize all custom element prop definitions (#14084)
cdec39afa fix: ensure custom element attribute/prop changes are in their own context (#14016)
06c9e5864 fix: exclude custom elements from HTML tree validation (#13540)
0466e4068 breaking: use `<svelte-css-wrapper>` instead of `<div>` for style props (#13499)
3d3006737 fix: mark custom element with virtual class attribute as dynamic (#13435)

content.text: Now let me check before this commit:

input.description: Check flush logic before simplify commit

content.text:
Now I understand! The logic is:
- If it's a custom element (`$$host` exists), DON'T call flushSync  
- If user explicitly sets `sync: false`, DON'T call flushSync
- Otherwise, call flushSync

But wait, the condition `if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)` means:
- If NOT custom element ($$host doesn't exist) OR sync is false, THEN flushSync

This is backwards! Let me think more carefully:
- `!options?.props?.$$host` = true when NOT a custom element
- `options.sync === false` = true when sync is false

So the condition says: flush when (NOT custom element) OR (sync is false)

But the comment says "We don't flushSync for custom element wrappers or if the user doesn't want it"

So it should be: flush when (NOT custom element) AND (sync is NOT false)

Actually let me re-read this. The condition is checking WHEN to flush:
```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flushSync();
}
```

This flushes when:
1. NOT a custom element (`!options?.props?.$$host` is true), OR
2. sync is explicitly false (`options.sync === false`)

But wait, that doesn't make sense. If `sync === false`, we shouldn't flush. Let me check what the original intention was by looking for when this was introduced:
Let me search the full git history:

<svelte:options customElement="host-styles-test" />

<script>
	import { onMount } from 'svelte';

	let Host = $host();

	// Track whether styles are present in $effect and onMount
	let effectHasStyles = false;
	let onMountHasStyles = false;

	$effect(() => {
		const innerHTML = Host.shadowRoot?.innerHTML || '';
		effectHasStyles = innerHTML.includes('<style>');

		// Attach results to the host for testing
		Host._effectHasStyles = effectHasStyles;
	});

	onMount(() => {
		const innerHTML = Host.shadowRoot?.innerHTML || '';
		onMountHasStyles = innerHTML.includes('<style>');

		// Attach results to the host for testing
		Host._onMountHasStyles = onMountHasStyles;
	});
</script>

<button class="btn">Test Button</button>

<style>
	.btn {
		width: 123px;
		height: 123px;
		background-color: red;
	}
</style>

content.text: Perfect! The test confirms the bug: `custom-elements > host-styles-sync` is failing with "style tag should be present in shadowRoot immediately after mounting". Now let's fix the bug:

Now let's fix the logic. The condition on line 126 should be:
- Flush if NOT in async mode
- AND NOT a custom element (no $$host)  
- AND sync is not explicitly set to false

The current condition is: `!async_mode_flag && (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)`

This says: flush if (NOT async mode) AND ((NOT custom element) OR (sync is false))

The correct condition should be: `!async_mode_flag && !options?.props?.$$host && options.sync !== false`

This says: flush if (NOT async mode) AND (NOT custom element) AND (sync is NOT false)

Perfect! So the commit 0672e4822 was first released in svelte@5.36.0, which matches the bug description.

Now, let me re-read the condition more carefully. The original bug is that custom elements SHOULD flush sync so that styles are available immediately. But the current code is NOT flushing for custom elements due to the faulty condition.

Let me re-check the original intention from the commit d8954d7ad:

The commit message says: "fix: ensure custom elements do not sync flush on mount"

But wait, that's contradictory to what the bug report says! Let me check the test from that commit more carefully:

I see - this test waits for a tick before the effect runs. So the original fix was to NOT flush sync for custom elements, allowing effects to run asynchronously.

But the bug report says that styles should be available in onMount/effect. Let me re-examine what's happening. The issue is that when the async_mode_flag was added, it broke the logic.

Let me trace through what happens:
1. Before commit 0672e4822: `if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)` - this would flush for NON-custom elements OR when sync is false (this is backwards!)
2. After commit 0672e4822: `if (!async_mode_flag && (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false))` - same condition but only when not in async mode

Wait, I think I've been misreading the condition! Let me re-analyze:

`if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)` means:
- if `$$host` doesn't exist (NOT a custom element), flush
- OR if `sync` is explicitly false, flush

But that doesn't make sense - why would we flush when sync is false?

Let me look at the history more carefully. Actually, let me check if there was a bug in commit 72b066b7f itself:

I see! The change in commit 72b066b7f introduced a bug! It changed from:
```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host) {
    flush_sync();
}
```

to:
```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flush_sync();
}
```

The comment says "We don't flush_sync for custom element wrappers or if the user doesn't want it", which suggests the intent was:
- Don't flush for custom elements
- Don't flush if user sets sync: false

But the code does the opposite! It FLUSHES when:
- NOT a custom element OR
- sync is false

It should be:
- Flush when NOT a custom element AND sync is NOT false

So my fix is correct! Let me verify by re-reading my edit:

```javascript
if (!async_mode_flag && !options?.props?.$$host && options.sync !== false) {
    flushSync();
}
```

This says:
- Flush when NOT in async mode AND NOT a custom element AND sync is NOT explicitly false

This matches the comment "We don't flushSync for custom element wrappers or if the user doesn't want it".

But wait, the bug report says styles SHOULD be available in custom elements. Let me re-read the original fix commit d8954d7ad again...

Actually, let me check if the issue is that custom elements SHOULD flush but currently don't. Let me look at the git history again to understand the original behavior:

Ah! Before d8954d7ad, it ALWAYS flushed sync, including for custom elements. Then d8954d7ad changed it to NOT flush for custom elements. So the original behavior was:
- Always flush (including custom elements)

Then d8954d7ad changed it to:
- Don't flush for custom elements

content.text:
So both commits have been there since 5.0.0! The bug is in 5.36.0+, so the issue must be with the addition of `async_mode_flag`. Let me check what changed. Looking back at the logic:

Before 0672e4822 (versions < 5.36.0):
```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flush_sync();
}
```

After 0672e4822 (versions >= 5.36.0):
```javascript
if (!async_mode_flag && (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)) {
    flushSync();
}
```

Ah! I think I've been misunderstanding the condition! Let me trace through more carefully.

For custom elements:
- `options?.props?.$$host` is truthy (the custom element itself)
- `!options?.props?.$$host` is false
- `options.sync` is undefined (not set)
- `options.sync === false` is false

So the condition `!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false` evaluates to `false || false` = `false`

Therefore, for custom elements, the condition is FALSE, so it does NOT flush!

Wait, that can't be right. Let me verify by checking if `$$host` is actually set:

Yes! For custom elements, `$$host: this` is passed as a prop. So when a component is a custom element:
- `options.props.$$host` is truthy (the custom element itself)
- `!options?.props?.$$host` is false

Given the condition `!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false`:
- For custom elements: `false || false` = false → does NOT flush
- For regular components: `true || false` = true → DOES flush

So custom elements were intentionally set to NOT flush sync since commit d8954d7ad. But the bug report says styles should be available. Let me re-read the bug description more carefully...

Oh wait! Maybe the bug isn't about WHETHER to flush, but WHEN the flush happens! Let me look at the async_mode_flag logic. Maybe in async mode, things are deferred?

Actually, let me re-check the commit 0672e4822 to see if async_mode_flag defaults to true or false:

So `async_mode_flag` defaults to `false`, but can be enabled. The condition is:
```javascript
if (!async_mode_flag && (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false)) {
    flushSync();
}
```

When async_mode_flag is false (default):
- For custom elements: `true && (false || false)` = `true && false` = `false` → does NOT flush
- For regular components: `true && (true || false)` = `true && true` = `true` → DOES flush

So the behavior is the same with or without async_mode_flag being false! The bug must be something else.


But wait, this condition is still wrong! Let me trace through it again more carefully:

For custom elements in 5.35.7:
- `options?.props?.$$host` = truthy (the host element)
- `!options?.props?.$$host` = false
- `options.sync` = undefined  
- `options.sync === false` = false
- Condition: `false || false` = false
- Result: Does NOT flush

So custom elements DON'T flush in 5.35.7 either! So how did it work?

Let me re-read the bug description. Maybe I'm misunderstanding. The user says "Component styles are not synchronously available when using the $host() rune within onMount."

Maybe the issue is that in 5.35.7, `queue_micro_task` ran before onMount/effect, but in 5.36.0+, it runs after?

Great! So `flush_tasks()` runs micro tasks. Let me check if this behavior changed:

Same! So `flush_tasks` hasn't changed. So when `flushSync()` is called, it should execute micro tasks which should include the `append_styles` queued micro task.

Wait, but for custom elements, `flushSync()` is NOT called! That's the issue. Let me re-read the legacy-client.js condition again:

```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flushSync();
}
```

For custom elements:
- `options?.props?.$$host` is truthy
- `!options?.props?.$$host` is false
- `options.sync === false` is false (sync is undefined)
- Condition: `false || false` = false
- Result: Does NOT call flushSync()

So custom elements DON'T call flushSync, which means micro tasks (including style injection) don't run immediately!

But the bug says it worked in 5.35.7. Let me create a simple test to verify my understanding. Actually, let me just check if maybe the bug report is wrong and it never worked? Or maybe the test expectation is wrong?

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the condition ONE MORE TIME very carefully. The condition is a DOUBLE NEGATIVE situation:

```javascript
// Comment says: "We don't flushSync for custom element wrappers or if the user doesn't want it"
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flushSync();
}
```

The comment says "we DON'T flushSync for X or Y", but then the code says "if NOT X or Y, THEN flushSync".

This is confusing! Let me trace it step by step:
- Comment intent: DON'T flush when (custom element) OR (sync is false)
- Code should be: flush when NOT ((custom element) OR (sync is false))
- Which is: flush when (NOT custom element) AND (NOT (sync is false))
- Which is: flush when (NOT custom element) AND (sync is NOT false)

But the code is:
```javascript
if (!options?.props?.$$host || options.sync === false) {
    flushSync();
}
```

Which means: flush when (NOT custom element) OR (sync is false)

This is BACKWARDS! The `||` should be `&&` and the `sync === false` should be `sync !== false`!

So the bug was introduced in commit 72b066b7f, not in commit 0672e4822!

content.text: The `effect-sequence` test passes, which was added in commit d8954d7ad to ensure effects run asynchronously. Let me check that test:

This test expects effects to run AFTER a tick, which means asynchronously. So the intention IS to not flush sync for custom elements.

So maybe the bug report is wrong, or the user's expectation is wrong? Let me re-read the bug carefully:

"Component styles are not synchronously available when using the $host() rune within onMount and $effect. This prevents reliable access to computed styles, dimensions or CSS variables of the host element immediately after it's mounted. This bug occurs in Svelte versions 5.36.0 through 5.36.13. Svelte 5.35.7 it behaves as expected."

The user says it worked in 5.35.7. But based on my analysis, the code in 5.35.7 should also NOT have styles available synchronously.

Unless... maybe the issue is not about custom elements but about regular components with injected CSS? Let me check if the test component I created is actually using injected CSS or not:

file.content:
<svelte:options customElement="host-styles-test" />

<script>
	import { onMount } from 'svelte';

	let Host = $host();

	// Track whether styles are present in $effect and onMount
	let effectHasStyles = false;
	let onMountHasStyles = false;

	$effect(() => {
		const innerHTML = Host.shadowRoot?.innerHTML || '';
		effectHasStyles = innerHTML.includes('<style>');

		// Attach results to the host for testing
		Host._effectHasStyles = effectHasStyles;
	});

	onMount(() => {
		const innerHTML = Host.shadowRoot?.innerHTML || '';
		onMountHasStyles = innerHTML.includes('<style>');

		// Attach results to the host for testing
		Host._onMountHasStyles = onMountHasStyles;
	});
</script>

<button class="btn">Test Button</button>

<style>
	.btn {
		width: 123px;
		height: 123px;
		background-color: red;
	}
</style>

content.text: So `onMount` is implemented as a `user_effect`! So it IS an effect. Let me check if `$effect` and `onMount` run in the same order:

So effects are deferred until mount! This means they don't run immediately during component creation.

Now, with my change to use `effect()` in `append_styles`, the style injection will also be deferred. So it should run at the same time as `$effect` and `onMount`.
